free_text: Activation and Angular Distribution Measurements
Of Li-7(P,N)Be-7 (0.0+0.429 MEV) For E(P)=25-45 MeV.
A Technique For Absolute Neutron Yield Determination.
Nekw
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Converted to Lower Case. Angular
Distributions
Of Neutrons are Added. See B0127003 and 004.
Last checking has been done.
The Given Cross Section For the Production of be-7
Results Exclusively from Reactions to the Ground
State Plus First Excited State (0.429 MEV), Because
These are the Only Particle-Emission-Stable States
Of be-7.
Li-7 Targets were Activated Both Singly and in
Multiple-Target Stacks. In the Latter Case the Mean
Proton Energy was Determined by Observing the Neutron
Time-Of-Flight Spectrum at 0 Degrees. Beam Currents
And Current Densities were As Low As 20 Nanoampere
And 250 Nanoampere/Cm**2 Respectively to Minimize
Target Heating. Aluminium Foils 8 Mg/Cm**2 Thick were
Placed After Each Target to Stop any be-7 Nuclei
Recoiling from the Target. The Targets were Mounted
In the Bottom of a Faraday-Cup of 1.3 Cm Diameter and
2.0 Cm Depth.
Li-7 Targets, Enrichment 99.99%, Thickness 20-60
Mg/Cm**2, Pressed Li-Metal.
The Total Error For the Given Cross Sections
Is +-3.6%. The Relative Error For Each Point is +-2%,
Resulting  from  the  Error  in Counting Statistics,
Uncertainties in Beam Energy and Solid Angle of Gamma
Counting, Change in Beam Flux Due to Reactions in the
Target Stack and Random Variations in Target
Thicknesses. An Additional Systematic Error of +-3% is
Due to the Efficiency Calibration of the Ge(LI)
Detector, Target Thickness (1%), and the Assumed Shapes
Of the Gamma Peak and Background.
Any Information For Uncertainty of Beam Energy
Is Absent For Original Compilation of Kachapag.
/Comment by CAJAD/
Table 2 of the Paper.

Production of be-7 Results Exclusively from Reactions

MEV), Because these are the Only
Particle-Emission-Stable States of be-7.
Flight Paths of 4-5 Meters.
To Eliminate Events Caused by Gamma Rays and
Alpha-Particles.
Thick Glass-Encapsulated Scintillation Detector
Containing Ne213 Liduid Scintillator.
Relative Error
Normalization Error.
The  Total Cross Section For the Li-7(P,N)Be-7 Reaction
Was Used For Absolute Normalization of Angular
Distributions.  By Observing the Produced be-7,
Absolute Neutron Fluence from the Reaction Li-7(P,N)
Can be Determined Independent of Beam Current and
Target Thickness Measurements.
Table 1 of Paper.
Flight Paths of 4-5 Meters.
